caption: Staff meeting ran 20 min over again. My brain is officially done for the day.
Was it the supply order thing again or did someone bring up the on-call schedule
On-call. Always on-call. Neil texted twice during it asking where the kids' sunscreen is.
lol the man cannot find anything in that house
Owen had it on his face when I left. Hazel had it in her hand. Figure it out, Neil.
ok but did you at least get the Thursday shift sorted
Moved to you. You mentioned you wanted it, confirmed with Sandra already.
yes perfect thank you. Drinks in Asheville soon, I genuinely need it

Staff meeting ran 20 min over again. My brain is officially done for the day.
Was it the supply order thing again or did someone bring up the on-call schedule
On-call. Always on-call. Neil texted twice during it asking where the kids' sunscreen is.
lol the man cannot find anything in that house
Chamomile finally flowering in the raised bed. Night Watch season is coming.
Dolly the sourdough starter turns four this month. Still cranking out a good crumb.
Craggy Gardens fog this morning. This is the reset I needed after a long call week.
Fresh Mountain Morning jars for Saturday market. Peppermint and lemon balm, hand-blended.
Owen found the mud puddle. Owen is the mud puddle now.
Nettle harvest before the rain. Gloves this time, learned my lesson.
Hazel drew the whole family as horses. I am the tall one, apparently.
Screened porch, drying herbs overhead, one cup of Night Watch. Off day earned.
French Broad greenway run before clinic. Cold but worth it.
Farmers market haul: bread, greens, and three conversations I didn't have time for.
New label design for the Foragers' Blend. Kraft paper and a little gold ribbon.
Tyler Childers on the porch speakers while I blend. Perfect Sunday.
Lemon balm taking over the garden. No complaints.
Max Patch summit. Wind trying to take my hat the whole way up.
Batch of Raspberry Leaf packed and shipped. Spring babies incoming for my customers.
Kale and white bean soup for the week. Meal-prep Sunday in full swing.
Loretta's chanterelles, courtesy of Ray's sharp eyes in the Roanoke woods.
Booth set up at the holiday craft fair. Come find the tea in the corner.
Morning yoga, twenty minutes, before the house wakes up.
Peppermint drying on the beams. The whole porch smells like Christmas.
Catawba Falls with the kids. Hazel counted forty-two steps, I lost count.
Jarred a new Elderflower Evening blend. Testing it on myself first.
Post-birth quiet. Sun coming up over West Asheville. Heading home to sleep.
Garden gate greens and a fresh pot of tea. This is the life I built.
Restocked the Etsy shop for the December rush. Small batch, as always.
